text.little_big_redstone.logic_config_bu: The number of inputs that this logic gate can accept.
The direction this port should interact with redstone power on.
Whether this port should input or output redstone power.
The direction this reader should read block capacity from.
The minimum percentage filled the block capacity must be for the output to be on.
The type of information to read from the adjacent block.
Whether the sequencer requires the input to be on to progress the timer.
The time (in ticks) before the output is on.
The time (in ticks) for the output to be on.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_and_: Output is ON when all inputs are ON, otherwise output is OFF.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_io_p: Can either input or output a redstone signal in the world on a single face. Multiple I/O ports can be used to input and output from different faces.
A microchip cannot have both an input and output port on the same face.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_nand: Output is OFF when all inputs are ON, otherwise output is ON.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_nor_: Output is ON when all inputs are OFF, otherwise output is ON.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_not_: Output is ON when the input is OFF, and output is OFF when the input is ON.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_or_g: Output is ON when any input is ON, otherwise output is OFF.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_read: Checks the filled percentage of an adjacent block and yields an ON signal if the block's filled percentage is greater than or equal to the set fill threshold.
Can be used on item, fluid, or energy storages.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_rs_n: When the reset (R) input is ON, the output is always OFF.
When the set (S) input is ON and the reset (R) input is OFF, the output is set to ON and will remain ON until the reset (R) input is ON.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_sequ: Delays the output signal by the set delay (in ticks).
The duration of the output signal can also be set by the duration (in ticks). A duration value of 1 means that the output will be a single tick.
Only an initial input ON signal is required to start the sequencer. However, if it is set to require continuous power to run, the sequencer will only progress if the input is currently ON.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_t_fl: When the input goes from OFF to ON, the output signal swaps states.
text.little_big_redstone.logic_help_xor_: Output is ON when the amount of ON inputs is odd, otherwise output is OFF.
